question.content: The chromophore of green fluorescent protein is formed from intramolecular cyclization, dehydration, and oxidation of amino-acid residues. When tyrosine is mutated to histidine, the absorption peak blue-shifts. Draw the corresponding chromophore skeletal structure (stereochemistry not required). Answer format: one final result only, in the form required by the problem; do not include any extra explanatory text. Answer format: one structural diagram only, with no extra text.

step_1.content: Identify the unmutated chromophore scaffold and isolate the side aromatic unit subject to substitution.
step_1.key_point: The imidazolinone core and methine bridge are preserved.

step_3.content: Replace the tyrosine phenyl unit with a histidine imidazole unit while preserving conjugation.
step_3.key_point: Only the left aromatic fragment changes from phenyl to imidazole.

step_5.content: Finalize the histidine-mutated GFP chromophore skeletal structure.
step_5.key_point: Target structure is the imidazole-substituted chromophore.
